novel_features.name: Pantry → recipe matcher
novel_features.description: Find Food52 recipes whose ingredients overlap your local pantry, ranked by coverage.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local pantry inventory joined against synced recipe ingredients — Food52's site only lets you filter by one ingredient at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user asks 'what can I make with what I have' rather than searching for one dish at a time.

novel_features.name: Offline FTS across recipes and articles
novel_features.description: Full-text search across every recipe and article you have synced, with type filtering.
novel_features.rationale: Requires recipes + articles in the local store; Food52's own search hits only one corpus at a time and needs a network round trip.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for lookups that can't justify a Typesense round trip or when offline.

novel_features.name: Sync a tag or vertical
novel_features.description: Pull recipes for one or more tags into the local FTS-indexed store.
novel_features.rationale: Foundation for offline pantry match and local search — not possible without synced state.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run before pantry match or offline search to seed the local cookbook.

novel_features.name: Test-Kitchen + rating-filtered browse
novel_features.description: Show only Food52 Test-Kitchen-approved recipes for a tag, with a rating floor.
novel_features.rationale: Uses Food52's testKitchenApproved + averageRating editorial signals that other recipe scrapers throw away. Food52's site has no first-class TK-only filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this over a broad search when the user wants 'a recipe Food52's editors signed off on,' not just any community recipe.

novel_features.name: Recipe scaling via JSON-LD
novel_features.description: Scale a recipe's ingredients to a different number of servings using its Schema.org recipeYield.
novel_features.rationale: Parses recipeYield + recipeIngredient from the page's JSON-LD and rewrites quantities. Food52's site has no scaler.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user is cooking for a different headcount than the recipe's default yield.

novel_features.name: Cooking-mode print view
novel_features.description: Render a recipe as ingredients + numbered steps with no nav, no images, no ads, no comments — ready to pipe to lp or paste into notes.
novel_features.rationale: The Food52 site's print button still loads ad chrome; the structured data lets us strip everything to just the cookable text.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user wants to actually cook from the recipe rather than browse it.

novel_features.name: Article ↔ recipe cross-reference
novel_features.description: Find synced articles that mention a given recipe in their relatedReading.
novel_features.rationale: Recipes link to articles, but never the reverse — we build the reverse index in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user wants the editorial context behind a recipe they've found.

narrative.headline: Search, browse, and read Food52 from your terminal — with offline FTS, pantry matching, recipe scaling, and the editorial signals other tools throw away.
narrative.value_prop: Every recipe and article on Food52, queryable without a browser. Ships with `pantry match` (find recipes from what you already have), `search` (offline FTS over your synced cookbook), `recipes top` (Test-Kitchen approved + rating-floored), and `scale` (resize ingredient lists via JSON-LD). The only existing Food52 CLI is a 2018-era Ruby HTML scraper that no longer runs against today's Vercel-protected site; this is a clean rebuild on Surf with Chrome TLS impersonation.
narrative.auth_narrative: No Food52 sign-in required. Food52 sits behind Vercel bot mitigation, but the challenge is passive (TLS-fingerprint), not JS-active — Surf with Chrome impersonation clears it without cookies or setup. Search uses a Typesense search-only key the CLI auto-discovers from Food52's public JS bundle, so users never see a key prompt or env var.

quickstart.comment: Full structured recipe (ingredients, steps, ratings, kitchen notes) for one recipe.
Seed the local store with two tags worth of recipes — required for offline search and pantry match.

quickstart.command: food52-pp-cli pantry add chicken garlic lemon thyme
quickstart.comment: Tell the CLI what's in your kitchen.
Find synced recipes you can mostly make right now.
Clean cooking-friendly view, ready to pipe to lp or paste.

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 429 or 'Vercel Security Checkpoint' on every request
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm you are running the printed CLI (which uses Surf + Chrome impersonation) and not your own curl. Plain HTTP is blocked by design; the CLI's transport handles it.

troubleshoots.symptom: recipes search returns 'Typesense key discovery failed'
troubleshoots.fix: Run `food52-pp-cli doctor` — it re-fetches the public _app.js bundle. Food52 rotates the bundle hash on each deploy; the CLI auto-recovers.

troubleshoots.symptom: recipes get returns 404 for a slug that exists
troubleshoots.fix: Food52 sometimes renames slugs. Try `recipes search '<title-fragment>'` to find the current canonical slug.
Run `sync recipes <tag>` first — pantry match operates on the synced store, not the live site. Try `food52-pp-cli search` to confirm sync wrote rows.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs Food52-quality recipes (community-curated, editor-tested) without rendering a browser or scraping HTML. The pantry match and offline FTS commands turn it into an edit-friendly cookbook the agent can keep on disk between sessions. Use the live `recipes search` for one-off lookups; sync + offline `search` for repeated queries against the same recipes.

recipes.title: Find a TK-approved chicken recipe under 5 ingredients
recipes.explanation: Typesense search filtered by tag, projecting just the fields an agent needs to pick a recipe.

recipes.title: Get the full recipe in JSON for downstream meal planning
recipes.explanation: Pulls structured ingredients + steps for piping into a meal planner or shopping list builder.

recipes.title: Build an offline weeknight cookbook in one shot
recipes.command: food52-pp-cli sync recipes weeknight quick-and-easy 30-minutes-or-fewer && food52-pp-cli search 'weeknight' --json
recipes.explanation: Pulls three high-signal tags into the local store, then queries via FTS5 — works on a plane.

recipes.title: What can I make right now?
recipes.explanation: Joins your local pantry against every synced recipe, returning the ones you can mostly make.

trigger_phrases: find me a food52 recipe for X
scale this food52 recipe to N servings
what can I cook from food52 with what's in my pantry
